description_long: This object consists of six cylindrical tubes, resembling sticks of dynamite, bundled together with two black straps. On one side, there is a visible electronic circuit board featuring a digital timer display, several colored wires, and a red button, suggesting a timed detonation mechanism. The tubes have a mottled, grayish-brown surface texture, giving them a rugged, industrial appearance. The wires are connected from the circuit board to the tubes, and the overall construction appears compact and purpose-built. The back side of the object is plain, showing only the bundled tubes and straps without any electronic components. The object is small enough to be carried by hand and is visually designed to evoke a classic improvised explosive device.
description: A bundle of six grayish dynamite-like tubes strapped together with a digital timer and visible wiring on one side. The object has a rugged, industrial look and compact form.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the open, circular ends of the six tubes arranged in a tight cluster, with no visible electronics.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the digital timer, circuit board, colored wires, and a red button attached to the bundled tubes.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view similar to view 0, showing the open ends of the tubes and some wiring along the side.
description_view_3: Rear side view showing only the bundled tubes and straps, with no visible electronics or timer.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The detonation mechanism appears to be a digital timer connected to the tubes via visible colored wires, with a red button likely serving as a trigger or arming switch.
